alerts.title: Explosions reported in Bandar Abbas; Iran's Mehr says situation 'under control'
alerts.summary: Reuters reports explosions heard in Iran, with state-affiliated Mehr agency stating the situation at Bandar Abbas — Iran's main port on the Strait of Hormuz and a critical naval/logistics hub — is 'under control.' The blasts come amid ongoing US-Iran deal talks Trump claims are 'largely negotiated' and follow Rybar reporting (~109K views) of continued US/Israeli strike preparation. No cause or casualty figures yet released.

alerts.title: Iran reportedly rebuilding IRGC missile 'cities' faster than US/Israeli estimates
alerts.summary: The Times of Israel cites assessments that Iran is reconstructing its ballistic missile production at a pace exceeding expectations. Rybar (~109K views) corroborates with US satellite-intelligence reporting that the IRGC has 'practically restored' underground missile cities whose tunnel entrances were damaged in earlier US and Israeli strikes. Combined with Iran's top security official vowing 'no retreat' (Iran International), the rebuild signals Tehran is preparing for a renewed kinetic phase rather than capitulation.

alerts.title: Houthi missile attack on Israel revives Red Sea shipping-strike fears
alerts.summary: AP News reports a fresh Houthi missile attack on Israel, with BBC, CFR and Global Security Review all flagging renewed risk to Red Sea commercial shipping. The strike comes with USS Eisenhower, USS Bataan, and three destroyers (Carney, Mason, Laboon) still concentrated in the southern Red Sea around 15-16°N. Houthi proxy status remains 'Elevated — Multiple Recent Attacks' in global metrics.

alerts.title: Iranian source: Khamenei sustained 'superficial' wounds in US-Israel strike
alerts.summary: Breakingthenews.net reports Iranian sources stating Supreme Leader Khamenei was physically wounded — described as superficial — in the earlier US-Israel strike campaign. The acknowledgment, if accurate, is the first confirmation that the strikes reached the Supreme Leader personally and reframes Khamenei's hardened red lines on uranium retention (previously reported by Reuters) as a personal as well as ideological stance.

alerts.title: Republican hawks publicly break with Trump over emerging Iran framework
alerts.summary: Middle East Eye reports Republican hawks attacking Trump's emerging Iran deal as a 'nightmare for Israel,' joining Israeli opposition leader Lapid's earlier criticism. The Jerusalem Post analysis argues the agreement 'weakens Iran but doesn't end its threat,' and The New Yorker frames any plausible deal as 'a humiliation.' US sentiment is 56% negative with 'criticism,' 'republican' and 'house' rising in the word cloud — indicating domestic political constraint on Trump's negotiating room.

analysis_note: Threat level holds at 9/10 with explosions reported in Bandar Abbas and Iran reportedly rebuilding missile production faster than expected, even as Trump publicly pursues a 'largely negotiated' deal that his own intelligence community, Republican hawks, and Tehran's security establishment are all signaling will not hold.
